# Painting { #tspaint.Painting }

```python
tspaint.Painting(
    posteriors,
    Q,
    pi,
    w,
    loglik_history,
    queries,
    ts=None,
    labels=None,
    default_deadband=0.0,
)
```

Result of :func:`paint`: the soft local-ancestry posteriors plus the fitted model.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**posteriors**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query haplotype, the down-pass posterior over ancestry states as contiguous :class:`~tspaint.output.Segment`\ s covering ``[0, L)`` (the soft, calibrated deliverable).

<code>[**Q**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The fitted generator.

<code>[**pi**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The fitted root frequencies.

<code>[**w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The learned per-tip credibility.

<code>[**loglik_history**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Observed-data log-likelihood per EM step (non-decreasing).

<code>[**queries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The painted sample-node ids.

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The tree sequence painted (retained so :meth:`rate_through_time` can reuse the fit; already in memory, so no extra cost).

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The reference labels used for the fit (retained for :meth:`rate_through_time`).

<code>[**default_deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Default dead-band passed to :meth:`segments`. Default ``0.0``.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [posterior_at](#tspaint.Painting.posterior_at) | Posterior vector for ``sample`` at a genomic ``position``. |
| [rate_through_time](#tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time) | Estimate the admixture (cross-ancestry) rate through time, reusing this fit. |
| [segments](#tspaint.Painting.segments) | Hard ancestry segments for downstream tract-length / dating analysis. |

### posterior_at { #tspaint.Painting.posterior_at }

```python
tspaint.Painting.posterior_at(sample, position)
```

Posterior vector for ``sample`` at a genomic ``position``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample-node id to look up.

<code>[**position**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Genomic position.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The ``(K,)`` posterior covering ``position``, or ``None`` if uncovered.

### rate_through_time { #tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time }

```python
tspaint.Painting.rate_through_time(edges=None, **kwargs)
```

Estimate the admixture (cross-ancestry) rate through time, reusing this fit.

Fits the time-inhomogeneous directional mugration EM
(:func:`tspaint.fit_rate_through_time`) **warm-started from this painting's fitted
``(Q, π, w)``**, so the homogeneous fit is not repeated. This is a *different
deliverable* from the painting — the cross-ancestry transition rates ``q_AB(t)``,
``q_BA(t)`` as functions of (backward) time, locating divergence / gene-flow epochs and
their direction. It returns a **new** :class:`~tspaint.dating.RateThroughTime` and does
**not** modify :attr:`posteriors` (CLAUDE.md: Q(t) gives no painting-accuracy gain, so the
paths stay side by side).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**edges**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array_like](`array_like`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Log-time grid edges; an auto grid is built from the node ages when ``None``.

<code>[****kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Forwarded to :func:`tspaint.fit_rate_through_time` (e.g. ``n_cells``, ``n_iter``, ``n_knots``).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tspaint](`tspaint`).[dating](`tspaint.dating`).[RateThroughTime](`tspaint.dating.RateThroughTime`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The directional rate-through-time profile (``.centers``, ``.q_AB``, ``.q_BA``, ``.plot()``).

### segments { #tspaint.Painting.segments }

```python
tspaint.Painting.segments(deadband=None)
```

Hard ancestry segments for downstream tract-length / dating analysis.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Confidence dead-band suppressing low-confidence flips that fragment long tracts. Defaults to :attr:`default_deadband`. See :func:`tspaint.output.hard_segments`.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query, hard ``(left, right, state)`` segments.
